tagline: Calculate batting average from hits and at-bats
keywords: batting average calculator, baseball batting average, hits at bats, baseball stats calculator
calcFn: var ba = hits / atBats; var rating = ba >= 0.300 ? 'Excellent' : ba >= 0.270 ? 'Good' : ba >= 0.250 ? 'Average' : ba >= 0.220 ? 'Below Average' : 'Poor'; var needed300 = Math.ceil(0.300 * atBats) - hits; document.getElementById('avg').textContent = '.' + Math.round(ba * 1000).toString().padStart(3, '0'); document.getElementById('rating').textContent = rating; document.getElementById('hitsNeeded').textContent = needed300 > 0 ? needed300 + ' more hits' : 'Already above .300!';
formulas: Walks, sacrifices, and hit-by-pitch are NOT counted as at-bats

faq.question: What is a good batting average?
faq.answer: In MLB, .300+ is excellent, .270-.299 is good, .250-.269 is average, and below .230 is poor. The overall MLB average hovers around .250. In Little League and high school, averages tend to be higher.

faq.question: What counts as an at-bat?
faq.answer: An at-bat is any plate appearance that results in a hit, out, error, or fielder's choice. Walks, hit-by-pitches, sacrifices, and catcher's interference are NOT counted as at-bats.

faq.question: Has anyone batted .400 in MLB?
faq.answer: Ted Williams was the last player to hit .400, batting .406 in 1941. No player has hit .400 since, making it one of baseball's most elusive achievements.

tagline: Calculate earned run average for pitchers
keywords: era calculator, earned run average, pitcher stats, baseball era calculator
calcFn: var e = (earnedRuns / inningsPitched) * 9; var rating = e <= 2.50 ? 'Ace/Elite' : e <= 3.50 ? 'Very Good' : e <= 4.00 ? 'Above Average' : e <= 4.50 ? 'Average' : e <= 5.00 ? 'Below Average' : 'Poor'; document.getElementById('era').textContent = e.toFixed(2); document.getElementById('rating').textContent = rating; document.getElementById('per9').textContent = e.toFixed(2) + ' ER per 9 innings';
formulas: Only earned runs count — runs from errors are excluded

faq.question: What is a good ERA?
faq.answer: In MLB: under 2.50 is elite, 2.50-3.50 is very good, 3.50-4.00 is above average, 4.00-4.50 is average, and above 5.00 is poor. In lower leagues, average ERAs tend to be higher.

faq.question: What is the difference between ERA and earned runs?
faq.answer: Earned runs are runs that score without the aid of fielding errors or passed balls. ERA normalizes this to a per-9-inning rate, allowing comparison between pitchers with different innings totals.

faq.question: What is the lowest ERA in MLB history?
faq.answer: The modern record (post-1900) is 0.96 by Dutch Leonard in 1914 (minimum 154 innings). Bob Gibson's 1.12 in 1968 is the most famous low ERA, which led to the lowering of the mound in 1969.

tagline: Calculate NFL passer rating from game stats
keywords: quarterback rating calculator, nfl passer rating, qb rating formula, passer rating calculator
calcFn: var a = Math.min(Math.max(((comp/att)-0.3)*5, 0), 2.375); var b = Math.min(Math.max(((yards/att)-3)*0.25, 0), 2.375); var c = Math.min(Math.max((td/att)*20, 0), 2.375); var d = Math.min(Math.max(2.375-((ints/att)*25), 0), 2.375); var pr = ((a+b+c+d)/6)*100; var grade = pr >= 100 ? 'Elite' : pr >= 90 ? 'Excellent' : pr >= 80 ? 'Good' : pr >= 70 ? 'Average' : 'Below Average'; document.getElementById('rating').textContent = fmt(pr, 1); document.getElementById('grade').textContent = grade; document.getElementById('compPct').textContent = pct(comp/att*100, 1); document.getElementById('ypa').textContent = fmt(yards/att, 1);

faq.question: What is a good NFL passer rating?
faq.answer: A rating of 100+ is elite, 90-99 is excellent, 80-89 is good, 70-79 is average, and below 70 is poor. The all-time record for a season is 122.5 by Aaron Rodgers in 2011. Perfect rating is 158.3.

faq.question: How is passer rating calculated?
faq.answer: The NFL formula uses four components: completion percentage, yards per attempt, touchdown percentage, and interception percentage. Each is scaled and capped at 2.375, then averaged and multiplied by 100.

faq.question: What is the difference between NFL and NCAA passer rating?
faq.answer: The NCAA uses a different formula that produces higher numbers (scale of 0-1000+). An NCAA rating of 150 is roughly equivalent to an NFL rating of 100. The two systems are not directly comparable.

tagline: Calculate projected fantasy points from player stats
keywords: fantasy football calculator, fantasy points calculator, fantasy football scoring, ppr calculator
calcFn: var std = (passYds * 0.04) + (passTD * 4) + (passInt * -2) + (rushYds * 0.1) + (rushTD * 6) + (recYds * 0.1) + (recTD * 6); var half = std + (receptions * 0.5); var full = std + (receptions * 1.0); document.getElementById('stdPts').textContent = fmt(std, 1) + ' pts'; document.getElementById('halfPpr').textContent = fmt(half, 1) + ' pts'; document.getElementById('fullPpr').textContent = fmt(full, 1) + ' pts';
formulas: Passing: 1 pt per 25 yards, 4 pts per TD, -2 per INT
Rushing: 1 pt per 10 yards, 6 pts per TD
Receiving: 1 pt per 10 yards, 6 pts per TD
PPR: +1 pt per reception, Half-PPR: +0.5 per reception

faq.question: What is PPR scoring?
faq.answer: PPR (Points Per Reception) awards 1 point for each catch. Half-PPR awards 0.5 per catch. This boosts the value of pass-catching running backs and slot receivers compared to standard scoring.

faq.question: How many fantasy points is a good game?
faq.answer: For QBs: 20+ is good, 30+ is great. For RBs/WRs: 15+ is good in standard, 20+ in PPR. A player averaging 15+ points per week in PPR is typically a reliable fantasy starter.

faq.question: Why do passing TDs only give 4 points?
faq.answer: Passing TDs award 4 points (vs 6 for rushing/receiving TDs) because QBs accumulate yards and TDs at a much higher rate. This balances QB scoring with other positions to maintain competitive leagues.

tagline: Calculate swimming pace per 100m from time and distance
keywords: swimming pace calculator, swim time calculator, swim pace per 100m, swimming split calculator
calcFn: var totalSec = minutes * 60 + seconds; var secPer100 = (totalSec / distance) * 100; var secPer50 = secPer100 / 2; var speedMph = (distance / totalSec) * 2.23694; var paceMin100 = Math.floor(secPer100 / 60); var paceSec100 = Math.round(secPer100 % 60); var paceMin50 = Math.floor(secPer50 / 60); var paceSec50 = Math.round(secPer50 % 60); document.getElementById('pace100').textContent = paceMin100 + ':' + paceSec100.toString().padStart(2, '0') + ' /100m'; document.getElementById('pace50').textContent = paceMin50 + ':' + paceSec50.toString().padStart(2, '0') + ' /50m'; document.getElementById('totalSec').textContent = minutes + ':' + seconds.toString().padStart(2, '0'); document.getElementById('speed').textContent = fmt(speedMph, 2) + ' mph';

faq.question: What is a good swimming pace per 100m?
faq.answer: Beginner: 2:30-3:00/100m. Intermediate: 1:45-2:15/100m. Advanced: 1:20-1:45/100m. Competitive: under 1:20/100m. Elite Olympic swimmers race at 0:55-1:05/100m for distance events.

faq.question: How do I improve my swimming pace?
faq.answer: Focus on technique first (streamlining, catch, rotation), then build endurance with interval sets. Drill work improves efficiency more than raw effort. Even 5-10% technique improvement can drop your pace significantly.

faq.question: How does pool length affect pace?
faq.answer: Short course (25m pool) times are typically 1-3% faster than long course (50m pool) due to more wall turns and push-offs. When comparing times, note whether it was short or long course.
